title: erysimum incanum subsp. aurigeranum
intro: Erysimum incanum subsp. aurigeranum, also known as the rock wallflower or mountain wallflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and relieve muscle spasms. The health benefits of Erysimum incanum subsp. aurigeranum include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a tincture, infused in teas, or applied topically as a poultice or ointment to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
